name: Frame Interpolation
description: Built-in Video Finish tool for post-generation or uploaded-source frame interpolation. Accepts Neo-owned generated results or browser-uploaded source videos and writes non-destructive child outputs.

labels.empty_state: Select a generated video result before running Frame Interpolation.
labels.missing_dependencies: Frame Interpolation needs VideoHelperSuite and ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation nodes. Base video generation is unaffected.
labels.readiness_unchecked: Readiness not checked. Check Admin > Extensions.

fields.label: VRAM Profile
fields.help_text: Medium is the stable default for 12GB-16GB GPUs. Profiles tune interpolation safety; they do not affect base video generation.

fields.label: Method
fields.help_text: Auto uses the safest detected stable method. RIFE is the recommended default for low/medium VRAM.

fields.label: Multiplier
fields.help_text: 2x is the stable default. Higher multipliers are heavier and should be validated locally.

fields.label: Output FPS
fields.help_text: Leave empty for backend/default behavior. Later phases may derive this from source FPS and multiplier.

fields.label: Output Format
fields.help_text: WebM remains the safest current default until MP4 handling is validated per backend.

fields.label: Clear Cache Every N Frames
fields.help_text: Advanced memory safety control. Lower values reduce OOM risk but can slow interpolation.

fields.label: Frame Load Cap
fields.help_text: Advanced cap for cautious/low-VRAM testing. Leave empty for the selected profile default.

slot_contract.rendering_rule: Render Frame Interpolation as the built-in direct card mounted inside the broader Video > Finish interpolation lane.
slot_contract.compatibility_rule: Keep video.finish.interpolate as the broad surface lane; keep video.finish.finish_interpolation as the extension-owned direct card slot.

vram_profile_contract.inheritance_rule: Custom starts from Medium defaults and only overrides explicitly changed advanced fields.
vram_profile_contract.dependency_rule: VRAM profiles tune interpolation params only; they must not add dependencies or block base Video Generation.
vram_profile_contract.fps_rule: When output_fps is empty or auto, backend should derive target FPS from source_fps * fps_multiplier to preserve clip duration.

low.label: Low VRAM
low.target: 8GB class GPUs, cautious systems, or unknown memory headroom
ui_warnings: Use short clips and lower resolutions first.
FILM/AMT stay hidden unless a later readiness phase explicitly promotes them.

medium.label: Medium VRAM
medium.target: 12GB-16GB GPUs; default local Neo workflow profile
medium.method_availability_rule: FILM and AMT may appear only after VFI readiness detection confirms their nodes.
ui_warnings: 4x is allowed only for short clips and should show a heavy-workload warning.
FILM/AMT are test methods, not defaults.

high.label: High VRAM
ui_warnings: Experimental TensorRT/GIMM/VSRFI methods remain diagnostics only until separate implementation phases.

custom.label: Custom
custom.target: Expert override profile for local validation and troubleshooting
ui_warnings: Custom does not bypass dependency readiness.
Custom must still create a child output and must never overwrite the source video.

admin_readiness_contract.admin_location: Admin > Extensions > Video > External Node Packs

controls_section.method_visibility: Show Auto/RIFE by default; show FILM/AMT only as stable optional methods after readiness detection.
controls_section.multiplier_policy: 2x stable default; 4x visible as warned/heavier where profile permits.

advanced_section.safety_rule: Advanced controls never bypass readiness gates or child-output-only policy.

inline_status_only_when_needed: Readiness not checked. Check Admin > Extensions.
Needs Frame Interpolation nodes. Check Admin > Extensions.

status_behavior.rendering_rule: Render status only when source/readiness/action state needs user attention; do not render permanent source-ready/readiness/child-output badges.
status_behavior.stale_result_rule: Ignore last interpolation status unless source_result_id and source_file_id match the active selected result/file.
allowed_copy: Readiness not checked. Check Admin > Extensions.
Needs Frame Interpolation nodes. Check Admin > Extensions.

source_picker_section.owner: Video Finish UI selection + backend payload

source_upload_policy.result_contract: uploaded video is registered as a Neo-owned Video ledger result before finish execution

rife_tensorrt.neo_policy: Detect in Admin only; never expose as stable Finish runtime method in V24.9.
risks: CUDA/TensorRT environment fragility
engine build/profile requirements
GPU/driver/Torch version mismatch risk
license review required before packaged recommendation
not suitable as default local 12GB workflow lane without validation

gimm_vfi.neo_policy: Detect in Admin only; never promote through VRAM profile selection in V24.9.
risks: CuPy/CUDA package matching is required
separate model/dependency lane from stable ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation
needs local 12GB validation before any runtime promotion

vsrfi_stream.neo_policy: Record as future research; keep out of stable Finish runtime and default UI choices.
risks: combined super-resolution and interpolation workflow is not the clean first interpolation lane
extra license/dependency/model-download policy needed
separate long-video routing phase required before runtime exposure

clean_ui_contract.reason: Remove diagnostic/source/profile noise from the creative Finish panel while preserving backend safety and Admin readiness ownership.
backend_policy_unchanged: source FPS preserved through backend normalization

node_requirements.missing_required_policy: Gate only Video > Finish > Frame Interpolation; never gate Video > Generation or WAN/LTX compile readiness.
node_requirements.missing_optional_policy: Hide or mark method-specific options as unavailable without disabling the stable RIFE lane when the stable lane is ready.
node_requirements.experimental_policy: Detected experimental packs may be reported in Admin diagnostics, but must not become defaults or auto-install targets in V24.2.
node_requirements.vram_profile_dependency_policy: VRAM profiles modify method/multiplier/cache/frame-window params only; they do not add installer behavior and must not gate base Video Generation.

notes: Stable first lane is RIFE VFI at 2x multiplier for low/medium VRAM.
The extension requires an existing Neo-owned source video and creates a child output.
Exact class-name detection remains owned by Video external node readiness, not raw manifest required_nodes.
V24.4 locks Medium VRAM as the stable default profile: RIFE, 2x, balanced cache, child output only.
V24.9 keeps RIFE TensorRT, GIMM-VFI, and VSRFI out of stable runtime despite Admin diagnostics.
Experimental method research does not change the V24.7 backend normalization or V24.8 output metadata schema.
Experimental methods must stay behind readiness diagnostics and should not be defaults.
TensorRT/GIMM/VSRFI need separate implementation phases before stable exposure.
V24.4 keeps experimental methods diagnostics-only; VRAM profile selection must not promote TensorRT/GIMM/VSRFI to default.
V24.9 documents experimental methods but does not promote them to runtime choices.
RIFE TensorRT requires separate CUDA/TensorRT validation and license review before any future runtime phase.
GIMM-VFI requires CuPy/CUDA matching and local VRAM validation before any future runtime phase.
VSRFI Stream is a future long-video Finish research lane, not a clean first-pass interpolation method.

rife_tensorrt.neo_policy: Detect in Admin only; never expose as stable Finish runtime method in V24.9.
risks: CUDA/TensorRT environment fragility
engine build/profile requirements
GPU/driver/Torch version mismatch risk
license review required before packaged recommendation
not suitable as default local 12GB workflow lane without validation

gimm_vfi.neo_policy: Detect in Admin only; never promote through VRAM profile selection in V24.9.
risks: CuPy/CUDA package matching is required
separate model/dependency lane from stable ComfyUI-Frame-Interpolation
needs local 12GB validation before any runtime promotion

vsrfi_stream.neo_policy: Record as future research; keep out of stable Finish runtime and default UI choices.
risks: combined super-resolution and interpolation workflow is not the clean first interpolation lane
extra license/dependency/model-download policy needed
separate long-video routing phase required before runtime exposure

v25_9_19_phase_2_status_behavior.ui_rule: small inline state only when needed

fixes: Map browser mp4/webm requests to VHS_VideoCombine format values video/h264-mp4 and video/webm.
Use frame_rate when VHS_VideoCombine exposes frame_rate instead of fps.
Populate required VHS_VideoCombine runtime inputs pingpong, loop_count, and save_output when present.
Select execution VFI nodes that accept IMAGE frames/images and avoid INTERP_MODEL loader nodes.
